rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NICOLAS GRANICH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/25/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The addresses of the conservatee's spouse and Catherine Sommer are different from those listed in the most recent contact information (filed 02/26/2020). Was notice properly given to them? 2) Furniture and personal items were listed on Attachment 1 of the inventory and appraisal. They were not appraised by the referee. 3) No proof of payment of the most recent assessment has been filed. 4) No credit union account statement has been filed for the period covering 11/30/2019. 5) The statement required by CRC 7.703(e)(4) for the paralegals' services is missing. 6) The summary of account must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form. CRC 7.575(a)(1). 7) Why was the IRA cash account appraised by the referee? Also, why is the beginning carry value the same as the account balance as of 09/30/2018? 8) Does the conservatee have a pension benefit plan in addition to the inventoried 401k plan? 9) Schedule A does not show any interest earned on the IRA account. 10) What were the terms of the loan to the conservatee's sister-in-law? 11) The 401k account statement shows four dividend payments on 08/31/2018 & 09/28/2018, but none of them were reported on Schedule A. 12) The two life insurance policies listed on Schedule C were not inventoried and appraised. 13) Was the conservatee paying the same premiums for both life insurance policies prior to the petitioner's appointment? 14) According to the inventory and appraisal, the referee commission was $1,384.27, but Schedule C shows a different amount. 15) Was the fair market value of the real property as of 11/30/2019 exactly the same as the carry value? 16) Schedule G1 shows the number of shares of each transaction, but the inventory and appraisal does not show the number of shares held as of 08/28/2018. 17) Did the petitioner request the distribution of $6,142.23 from the 401k plan reported on Schedule G2? If so, did the petitioner obtain court authorization? 18) Did the petitioner obtain court authorization to request the RMD from the IRA reported on Schedule G2? 19) What were the in-out times of the petitioner's attorney's court appearance on 10/18/2018? 20) Is the conservatee's real property vacant? Is the conservatee's nephew occupying the property? 21) Are all the assets listed on the inventories and appraisals the conservatee's separate property or his one-half share of community property? How did the petitioner make the characterization of each asset?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GAEHWILER 2000 TRUST under agreement dated 12/11/00, as amended
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: An appearance is required. The Court is to determine whether the subject bank accounts should be confirmed as assets of the trust. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLYDE SYDNEY WHITMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order for information regarding remote appearances.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN JOSEPH GRIFFIN JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/01/2020. Examiner notes emailed 07/31/2020. Issues: 1) The Declaration setting forth the anticipated liabilities of the decedent for the court to determine bond must be signed and verified by the petitioner. (CRC 7.103). 2) Please explain why the petitioner is requesting $20,000.00 in bond when the unsecured liabilities are $71,077.06. The bond amount will be rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars. [LRSF 14.44] 3) A hard copy of the proposed order must be dropped off at the Superior Court in room 202. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: FRANCESCO MARIO TUFO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order (on the court's website, on the probate division page) for information on how to appear remotely by zoom (free) or courtcall (fee). please note, the examiner attempted to email petitioner using the email address on the pleadings, but it was returned as undeliverable.
